item.title: Trump threatens to put ICE agents in airport security unless Democrats end shutdown
item.description: US President Donald Trump has threatened to deploy ICE agents to US airports if congressional Democrats do not agree to fund airport safety immediately.

item.title: When is location sharing a red flag in relationships?
item.description: Experts say sharing where you are with a partner using technology should be mutual and enhance a feeling of safety.

item.title: Cuba's Castro dynasty could shape US crisis talks as Australian diaspora awaits Trump 'takeover'
item.description: Former Cuban president Fidel Castro's enigmatic lineage could form part of Donald Trump's shadowy plans to "take" or strike a deal with the Caribbean nation.

item.title: '25 years is long enough': Scott won't ask for patience from Bombers fans
item.description: West Coast wins its first match since round 11 last year while the Bombers sink to a second straight crushing loss to start the 2026 season.

item.title: Hardcore band Parkway Drive distances itself as former merchandise manager pleads guilty in court
item.description: Hardcore music megastars Parkway Drive are distancing themselves from their former merchandise manager, who has pleaded guilty to having sexual intercourse with a minor.

item.title: Socceroos recall for Awer Mabil sends message ahead of FIFA World Cup
item.description: The recall of forgotten Socceroo Awer Mabil is proof Tony Popovic places no limit on how wide he casts his FIFA World Cup selection net, says Australia defender Jason Geria.

item.title: Anthony Albanese urges 'vigilance' against those seeking to 'turn back the clock' on Australia
item.description: Anthony Albanese says the country must be "vigilant" against politicians seeking to return to an era of Australia that was less accepting of migrants, following the South Australian election where One Nation overtook the Liberal Party on a platform opposing multiculturalism.

item.title: Eels coach calls out NRL rules after key forward suffers torn ACL
item.description: Parramatta Eels forward J'maine Hopgood suffers an ACL injury in a victory over Dragons, leaving coach Jason Ryles to lament the NRL's insistence on speeding up the game.

item.title: Alana King takes two wickets as Australia defeats West Indies in women's T20 series
item.description: Alana King adds more intrigue to Australia's spin dilemma, strengthening her T20 World Cup claims with two more wickets to save her side from an upset loss to West Indies.

item.title: Matildas rally behind Caitlin Foord as Sam Kerr faces club limbo after Asian Cup defeat
item.description: The Matildas have backed Caitlin Foord to overcome her Asian Cup heartache as questions continue to swirl about the future of captain Sam Kerr.

item.title: Cricket Australia releases full international schedule for the 2026/2027 summer
item.description: Cricket Australia has been forced into starting the core part of its Test summer in Perth on a Wednesday, as a result of the most congested schedule in the sport's history.

item.title: Microsoft's default wallpaper Bliss, the most viewed photograph in history, turns 30 this year
item.description: Plastered across screens in offices, schools and homes globally, Bliss is one of the most well-known images in the world.

item.title: Markets are complacent as Iran war threatens global economy
item.description: The markets' benign assessment of the Iran war is wrong and the damage to the global economy could get worse.

item.title: South Australian election shows One Nation threatens Liberals and Labor
item.description: One Nation delivers its best electoral result in nearly 30 years, demonstrating that opinion polls showing its vote rising have not been a political beat-up but a real electoral phenomenon that will lead to a political reckoning in Australian politics.

item.title: Home building industry facing COVID-like price spikes due to Middle East war
item.description: From sand to concrete, suppliers are bringing in "emergency fuel levies" as petrol prices soar due to the war in the Middle East. Major companies that supply pipes for plumbing are even warning of shortages.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: The Matildas need a grand plan to stave off end-of-an-era concerns
item.description: The Women's Asian Cup has shown that without a grand plan for the future of women's football and a willingness to stick to it, the Matildas may never capture an elusive major trophy again.

item.title: Why Ghanim named his robotic arms after the Peaky Blinders brothers
item.description: Ghanim Al Shnen became a pioneer in the use of mind-controlled robotic limbs after losing both his arms. Although he would never have chosen this fate, it ultimately gave him the life for which he longed.

item.title: Shipping boss pushes for new Bridport channel entrance to stop his ship running aground
item.description: David Harris's ship bottomed out with 400 cattle on board at the end of last year, leading to a 12-hour wait for the next high tide. He wants a solution.

item.title: Disbelief and praise from teenagers and parents as Indonesia's social media ban set to take effect
item.description: In a country with 70 million children, some question how the incoming social media ban in Indonesia will be effectively implemented, while others welcome the new restrictions.

item.title: Private health insurers expand into GP clinics, telehealth, prompting calls for more oversight
item.description: Increasing partnerships between health insurers, GP centres and telehealth services has raised concerns about a potential two-tier system in which privately insured patients have more affordable access to GP consultations.

item.title: Why Brumbies skipper Ryan Lonergan is crucial to his side's Super Rugby Pacific championship hopes
item.description: Brumbies captain Ryan Lonergan led by example in the come-from-behind win over the Chiefs, underlining how vital he is to his side's finals hopes.

item.title: Barking dogs, overhanging trees, shared fences — how to resolve issues with your neighbour
item.description: Someone feeling like their neighbour is intruding on them in "either psychological or practical ways" tends to be at the heart of most disputes. So if you're having an issue with the people next door, what's the best way to approach it?

item.title: Donald Trump orders ICE agents to man US airport security checkpoints
item.description: A fight over funding for the Department of Homeland Security leads to lengthy queues at US airports.

item.title: Few leaders in Australia are as firmly entrenched in power as Peter Malinauskas
item.description: After Saturday's landslide, Premier Peter Malinauskas has more political capital than any South Australian Labor leader in modern history.

item.title: Government agency found fuel supply crisis would have 'significant impact' months before Iran war
item.description: Nine months before the Iran war oil crisis, the federal government was warned of the "fragility" of Australia's critical infrastructure if a fuel crisis took hold.

item.title: Supreme Court case highlights how councils use intimidation to coerce homeless people into eviction
item.description: A recent Supreme Court case has shed light on how councils coerce homeless people into eviction.

item.title: This week in history quiz, March 23
item.description: Can you remember who Will Smith slapped, or what happened during "sandpapergate" scandal? Test yourself with today's quiz.

item.title: Cheryl Grimmer's UK cousin prompts authorities to review decades‑old case
item.description: Michael Grimmer challenges the 2019 court ruling that excluded a key confession in his cousin Cheryl Grimmer's 1970 disappearance, leading to a major breakthrough in the case.

item.title: Bowen confirms petrol stations on east coast experiencing shortages — as it happened
item.description: Energy Minister Chris Bowen has confirmed dozens of petrol stations on Australia's east coast are facing shortages amid a Question Time grilling on fuel supplies.

item.title: How living with a disability in regional Australia poses extra challenges
item.description: NDIS recipients living in regional Australia are calling for the government to provide better care for those who live remotely with travel costs often eating up a large portion of their support budgets.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: How Sam Kerr, Alanna Kennedy and every other Matildas player fared at the tournament
item.description: From the players who lit up the tournament to those who never quite got going, here is a closer look at how each Matilda fared at the 2026 Women's Asian Cup.

item.title: Perth and Mandurah youth homelessness hub Passages sees more demand amid housing crisis
item.description: Service providers like Passages in Perth and Mandurah help hundreds experiencing homelessness during the day, but concern remains amid a shortage of beds across the sector.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands launches legal action against ARN Media for termination of $100m contract
item.description: Controversial radio host Kyle Sandilands has launched legal action against ARN Media after it terminated his $100 million contract.

item.title: Doomadgee flood isolation to enter fourth month as fuel, food run low
item.description: The Aboriginal Gulf of Carpentaria community of Doomadgee is entering its fourth month of flood isolation, as residents prepare for another month surrounded by rising rivers.

item.title: Underground 'bush doofs' get legal makeover in WA's South West
item.description: Margaret River promoter Muso Kami hopes to ease local concerns and provide a place for people to dance the night away without breaking the law.

item.title: Talia Gibson downs Iva Jovic at Miami Open to set up Elena Rybakina showdown
item.description: Talia Gibson's run at Indian Wells carried her to a career-high WTA ranking, and now she is on a similar charge in Miami, with world number two Elena Rybakina up next.

item.title: Corentin Moutet denies Danielle Collins's claim he slid into her DMs
item.description: Corentin Moutet chides the Tennis Channel after Danielle Collins claimed on-air that he slid into her DMs after she said she did not date "short kings".

item.title: Albury Wodonga Health CEO resigns from hospital board amid staff concerns over safety
item.description: Albury Wodonga Health CEO Bill Appleby resigns after several recent votes of no confidence in the hospital's executive leadership.

item.title: One Nation's support has soared in South Australia, and voters explain their reasons
item.description: Heading into the South Australian election, One Nation was backing its chances to pick up votes from across the political spectrum, and supporters say various factors informed their decisions.

item.title: Search resumes for man missing off Sussex Inlet on NSW South Coast
item.description: Police and emergency service crews are continuing the search for a 25-year-old man who went missing in water near Sussex Inlet.

item.title: Doctors say government ignoring scientific evidence in favour of 'ideology' in move to scrap drug diversion laws
item.description: The state government is proposing to scrap drug diversion laws, which provide a three strikes system before criminal charges, in a crackdown on "addiction and criminal offending".

item.title: Jarome Luai set to miss four weeks, Isaiah Iongi handed NRL breach notice, Ryan Couchman sent to judiciary
item.description: Jarome Luai will miss a month with his knee injury, but his Wests Tigers halves partner Adam Doueihi has been cleared of a hamstring issue and is on track to play in the club's next game.

item.title: ABC staff vote 'No' to latest enterprise agreement offer, triggering strike action
item.description: Staff were informed this morning that a majority of ABC workers had voted against the broadcaster's latest enterprise agreement offer.

item.title: Port Adelaide captain Connor Rozee faces surgery, Gold Coast Suns to fight AFL ban for Bailey Humphrey
item.description: Port Adelaide captain Connor Rozee faces a major disruption to his AFL season after scans confirmed he sustained a high-grade hamstring tendon rupture.

item.title: Choking charge dropped against Perth father Steven James Naoum after apology
item.description: Perth orthodontist Steven James Naoum walks free from court as a charge alleging he choked an 11-year-old boy at a junior soccer match is dismissed.

item.title: Pedestrian injured after woman falls from Melbourne building
item.description: A man is hospitalised with serious injuries after being hit by a woman who police believe fell from a building in Melbourne's CBD.

item.title: International Energy Agency says second round of emergency fuel releases is on table
item.description: World leaders have failed to grasp the depth of the energy crisis created by the Iran war, the head of the International Energy Agency has warned, as he considers releasing extra emergency oil stores.

item.title: Man who killed partner Nikkita Azzopardi found dead in Melbourne jail cell
item.description: The grieving family of a Melbourne woman who was bashed to death say they felt relieved when they learned her killer died in jail over the weekend.

item.title: Make-ahead breakfast ideas for a stress-free morning routine
item.description: From breakfast sandwiches to chia pudding, these delicious morning options can be prepared ahead for a stress-free start to the day.

item.title: Electric highway issues causing range anxiety for EV drivers crossing Nullarbor
item.description: Constant malfunctions, long repair times and inadequate power supply are causing some EV drivers to rethink long trips in Western Australia.

item.title: What's the price where you are? Share your servo pics with us
item.description: We want to know how much fuel costs where you are. Send us your photos.

item.title: Cricket Australia boss Todd Greenberg says New Zealand's BBL rejection is not a surprise
item.description: New Zealand's rejection of an offer to join the BBL did not surprise Cricket Australia's boss.

item.title: Nurses' union claims Tasmanian government policy has contributed to patient deaths
item.description: The Tasmanian nurses' union says a policy that gives paramedics an hour to transfer the care of their patients to an emergency department has led to patient deaths. The state government says there's no evidence to support the claim.

item.title: ASX falls, Asia tumbles, gold crashes as Trump keeps markets on edge
item.description: The Australian share market fell, Asian shares were generally clobbered and precious metals were a long way off earlier peaks. But it could have been worse.

item.title: Farmers warn of food price spikes within weeks as Middle East war continues
item.description: Australia's peak farming body is warning consumers the price of groceries is about to jump as producers and the freight industry grapple with diesel price hikes and supply issues.

item.title: Perth parents frustrated by difficulties accessing specialist epilepsy care for their son
item.description: A Perth mother whose three-year-old son has a complex form of epilepsy, believes the health system is failing her son because he is being pushed between two public hospitals.

item.title: Grandfather clock, jewellery box in alleged $148k theft by former National Trust boss
item.description: Matthew Paul Smithies was charged with the alleged thefts in 2023. Until now, a suppression order has prevented the media from reporting on the case, which remains before the Supreme Court.

item.title: Monday Finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: St John WA reveals rise in assaults on paramedics as officer details incidents that left her 'traumatised'
item.description: An ambulance officer details her harrowing experiences on the front line, as St John WA reports a rise in violence and aggression towards its staff.

item.title: Water Corporation's revised bridge limits worry South West WA farmers
item.description: A decision by WA's state-owned water utility to cut weight limits on bridges it controls is causing disruption for farmers and businesses south of Perth.

item.title: Tasmania Devils make their VFL debut
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Mannix starts Mums WhatsApp group, Wallam's revenge and Housby back to her best
item.description: Emily Mannix talks about her extended absence from the Super Netball league, and why she's now ready to return for the Melbourne Vixens after a "freak accident" meant her daughter Mabel needed emergency care at birth.

item.title: Buying a share of my in-laws' home helped us into the property market
item.description: Experts say housing affordability is pushing people to rethink the rule book on property ownership.

item.title: Donald Trump says US and Iran 'want deal' as Tehran calls claim of talks 'fake news'
item.description: Donald Trump says there have been "major points of agreement" in US-Iran talks to end the war. But Iran denies those claims.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz remains blocked by Iran while zombie ships sail through
item.description: Iran has effectively blocked the only way out of the Persian Gulf, leaving about 3,000 ships stranded. But two zombie ships appear to have sailed in and out of the Gulf through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Rising fuel prices potentially forcing rural patients to forgo medical appointments, experts warn
item.description: The Rural Doctors Association says some patients may be forced to make "life and death" decisions due to the rising cost of fuel in regional Australia.

item.title: New aged care algorithm under fire as 800 apply for review
item.description: A new algorithm for deciding how much aged care support people can receive to stay living at home is being blamed for reducing care to older Australians.

item.title: Donald Trump emboldens TACO taunts over sudden Iranian strikes backdown
item.description: After threatening to "obliterate" Iran's power plants, Donald Trump's sudden backdown is again fuelling TACO taunts.

item.title: Australia's fuel security is exposed by Iran war but renewable energy offers an out
item.description: From UN leaders to former Australian Defence Force chiefs, a growing chorus of voices argues that dependence on fossil fuels is not just a climate issue, but a national security risk — exposing countries to volatile prices and supply shocks.

item.title: Disillusioned voters drive One Nation surge as most Australians feel ignored by politicians
item.description: Civic groups call for sweeping reforms to restore falling trust, which experts have linked to a resurgence of populist "protest" voting at the expense of the major parties.

item.title: Gambling companies targeting women with 'novel' promotions, new research finds
item.description: Gambling is being promoted as fun, social and "harmless" for women through social media and lifestyle marketing, in a shift researchers warn is normalising betting and obscuring the risks.

item.title: Headbutting sperm whales filmed on drone cameras
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Prosecco to be protected under Australia and European Union free trade agreement
item.description: Australia's right to make and sell prosecco is expected to be protected under a long-awaited free trade pact set to be signed with the European Union after almost a decade of negotiations.

item.title: Ashton Hurn played a short and difficult game ahead of the SA election
item.description: Sport and politics are similar. Good players work hard and perform well under pressure, something SA Liberal Leader Ashton Hurn has been credited for. But the party's disastrous election results show just how difficult the game can be.

item.title: New Hong Kong law gives police powers to demand phone and computer passwords
item.description: People in Hong Kong now face a year in jail if they refuse to hand over their phone or computer passwords to police.

item.title: Pakistan prepared to host US-Iran peace talks led by JD Vance, officials say — as it happened
item.description: A Pakistani official says direct talks between the US and Iran could be held in Islamabad this week, with Vice President JD Vance set to meet with Iranian officials. As it happened.

item.title: Children to be banned from riding e-bikes and e-scooter devices in Queensland under proposed laws
item.description: A raft of new rules around the use of e-mobility devices will be ushered in after the Queensland government accepted the recommendations from a parliamentary inquiry.

item.title: The Kyle and Jackie O Show axed 'prematurely' by ARN Media, employment lawyer says
item.description: A Sydney-based employment lawyer says the network who owns radio station KIIS FM acted "prematurely" when it cancelled the controversial Kyle and Jackie O Show.

item.title: Trump looks increasingly trapped over Iran as markets gyrate and oil shortage hits heartland
item.description: Donald Trump appears to be fast running out of exits on his Iran war folly.

item.title: Women are less likely than men to receive CPR. This surf club is looking to change that
item.description: NSW Ambulance data shows women are 10 per cent less likely than men to receive CPR from a bystander in public, and 50 per cent less likely to get defibrillation .

item.title: Retirees grow native forest from scratch on Tasmanian conservation covenant
item.description: Some become grey nomads, others learn a language or some other skill. After retirement, this couple took on creating a native forest. But, as John and Annette were to find out, there is "no textbook".

item.title: Australian Youth Orchestra shows the connection between maths and music at World Science Festival
item.description: The Australian Youth Orchestra brings music, maths and science together at the World Science Festival, revealing how patterns and numbers are connected to the sounds we hear.

item.title: England completes post-Ashes review as Brendon McCullum and Ben Stokes remain coach and captain
item.description: The England and Wales Cricket Board has completed its review into a disastrous tour of Australia and New Zealand, with coach Brendon McCullum, captain Ben Stokes and men's team director Rob Key all keeping their jobs.

item.title: Audio recording reveals moments after fatal LaGuardia airport crash
item.description: Audio recordings from New York's LaGuardia airport reveal an air traffic controller appearing to admit he "messed up" just moments after an Air Canada Express regional jet crashed into a fire truck, killing the pilot and co-pilot.

item.title: Diesel-reliant remote communities left vulnerable amid Iran conflict
item.description: Remote communities' reliance on diesel fuel for power leaves them vulnerable in a global crisis.

item.title: Blow to Italian PM Giorgia Meloni as she suffers referendum defeat
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni concedes defeat in a referendum on justice reform, but despite the major blow to her far-right leadership, she insists she is going nowhere.

item.title: Eli Katoa's NRL future uncertain after head knocks, Melbourne Storm coach Craig Bellamy says
item.description: Melbourne Storm coach Craig Bellamy says it is too early to know if superstar back rower Eli Katoa will get back on the field in 2027 or at all.

item.title: Alice Springs residents conflicted over Pine Gap's role in US strikes in Middle East
item.description: Residents with family in the Middle East are grappling with the part the highly secretive Pine Gap base may be playing in the Iran war from their own backyard.

item.title: Bill Cosby ordered to pay $US19m for historical sexual assault
item.description: A civil jury in California awards a woman $US19.25 million after finding Bill Cosby liable for sexually assaulting her.

item.title: Hezbollah serves as 'pretext' for Israeli attacks, Lebanon's Justice Minister Adel Nassar says
item.description: Lebanon's justice minister accuses the Iranian-backed militant group Hezbollah of effectively giving Israel an excuse to attack the country amid threats of an Israeli occupation of the south.

item.title: Deputy premier defends choice of Fitzroy River for 2032 Olympic Games despite growing concerns
item.description: Jarrod Bleijie remains adamant that a controversial central Queensland river chosen as a venue for the 2032 Brisbane Games will host rowing and canoeing events.

item.title: Handball champions Füchse Berlin find Bundesliga trophy in basement after reporting it stolen
item.description: A German handball trophy believed to have been stolen and melted down is found in the basement of the team that won it.

item.title: Regional WA ambulance volunteers receive 'unfathomable' donation from patient
item.description: A volunteer ambulance service in regional WA has received a donation from a thankful patient of more than $280,000, allowing it to purchase a new ambulance.

item.title: Donald Trump's Greenland threat likely to salvage Danish PM's political future
item.description: When Donald Trump threatened to annex Greenland, Denmark leader Mette Frederiksen was flailing in the polls. Having stood up to the US president, she looks to have salvaged her political career.

item.title: Donald Trump's backtrack illuminates Iran's leverage in an asymmetrical war
item.description: In the wake of Donald Trump's backtrack, Iran realises more clearly than ever that it wields a potent new weapon on the global stage.

item.title: Company that built highly criticised BOM website wins $16m contract for new site
item.description: The company responsible for the highly criticised BOM website wins a $16 million tender to build a new climate data website for the government.

item.title: Disability and chronic pain made motherhood isolating for Megan
item.description: Megan Blin says her chronic pain made it difficult to do things other first-time mums could.

item.title: Japan urges public not to panic-buy toilet paper amid Iran war
item.description: Japan's government releases a statement assuring people there is no issue with supply and no need to stock up, despite "reports and social media posts regarding the distribution of toilet paper".

item.title: Influencer Stacey Warnecke's 'birthkeeper' may be compelled to give evidence at inquest, court hears
item.description: A coroner investigating the death of nutrition influencer Stacey Warnecke after a freebirth may compel her support worker to appear at the inquest.

item.title: CCTV shows plane slamming into fire engine on airport runway
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Anthony Monteleone stabbed ex-girlfriend in car park after she ended relationship, jury hears
item.description: Anthony Monteleone allegedly intended to kill his former girlfriend after realising his repeated, unwelcome attempts to persuade her against ending their short-term relationship were futile, a jury has heard.

item.title: Iran shows Europe 'in reach' with longest-range ballistic missile launch
item.description: An attempted attack on a US-UK military base in the Indian Ocean by Iran shows Tehran can launch missiles as far as 4,000km, raising concerns European cities are in reach.

item.title: Beef and lamb exporters label EU free trade agreement 'simply appalling'
item.description: The National Farmers' Federation says the deal will leave farmers without meaningful access to the EU market.

item.title: Georgia Voll hits Australia's third fastest T20 ton in victory over West Indies
item.description: Georgia Voll puts her name alongside the likes of Alyssa Healy and Meg Lanning after smashing a 52-ball hundred in Australia's win over West Indies.

item.title: Pastoral company fined record $45,000 for Queensland biosecurity breaches
item.description: A pastoral company has been hit with a record $45,000 fine after moving thousands of cattle across state lines without proper tracking. Authorities say the penalty sends a clear warning.

item.title: Longest-running abalone farm in the Southern Hemisphere to shut due to harmful algal bloom
item.description: Fifteen permanent jobs will be lost on Kangaroo Island with the closure of Yumbah Aquaculture's abalone farm.

item.title: Audiences consider The Diary of Anne Frank an 'uplifting' story arriving in Sydney at the right time
item.description: While considering it a "big responsibility" bringing the theatre production to Sydney at this time, actress Chloe-Jean Vincent says Anne Frank's story makes theatregoers feel "inspired and so uplifted".

item.title: Talia Gibson's dream US run ended by world number two Elena Rybakina in Miami Open
item.description: Aussie Talia Gibson will leave the US full of pride and confidence despite falling to Elena Rybakina in the fourth round of the Miami Open.

item.title: Outback town of Marree receives anonymous donation of supplies after being cut off by floodwater
item.description: An anonymous donor funded two planes full of supplies for the outback town of Marree, near Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre, after it was cut off by flooding.

item.title: How Bron Lewis went from dingy bars to hosting a MICF gala
item.description: The crimson-haired comedian has gone from tiny open mics to the biggest stage in Melbourne in just a few short years.

item.title: Proposed Aboriginal land rights law change limits unused Crown land access, councils say
item.description: Aboriginal councils who have fought for decades to secure ancestral land are concerned proposed law reform puts rights at risk.

item.title: EU leader urges democracies to band together in 'upside down' world
item.description: The Europe Commission president declares that Europe and Australia must rearm and decarbonise as they try to break economic and energy dependencies on China and Russia.

item.title: Iranians celebrate ancient Nowruz despite repression and war with US, Israel
item.description: Amid war and repression, Iranians cling to hope with the celebration of Nowruz, the pre-Islamic tradition symbolising renewal.

item.title: Organised criminals increasingly working with foreign 'despots and dictators', AFP boss warns
item.description: The Australian Federal Police is warning that foreign intelligence agencies are increasingly working with locally-based organised criminals to carry out "serious crimes", including terrorism, within Australia.

item.title: Cast in Blue googly eyes
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Mount Gambier woman convicted of graffitiing Cast in Blue sculpture
item.description: A 20-year-old woman who made headlines by putting googly eyes on a controversial sculpture in the middle of Mount Gambier is ordered to pay $2,000 to the city's council.

item.title: Oil price rises, Australian dollar falls as Strait of Hormuz remains effectively shut
item.description: A key factor for shares, bonds, commodities and currencies remains consistently unchanged — the Strait of Hormuz is still effectively closed.

item.title: Australia's diesel standards lowered for six months as hundreds of petrol stations run out of certain fuels
item.description: Australia is temporarily lowering standards for diesel to allow more supply into the domestic market as service stations across the country continue to experience localised shortages.

item.title: Alex Robertson says Socceroos allegiance was never in doubt as he pushes for World Cup selection
item.description: Alex Robertson says his allegiance to the Socceroos was never in doubt, as he pushes to earn selection in Tony Popovic's World Cup squad.

item.title: Reducing 'just in case' purchases can help avoid empty shelves and fuel bowsers
item.description: If you've topped up your tank at a petrol station recently did it feel like you were "panic buying"?

item.title: Brazilian man Marcelo Peres-Colhado, accused of being part of car theft syndicate, denied bail
item.description: The prosecution argued the man was in Australia on an expired visa and could face deportation if granted bail.

item.title: Labor caucus chooses new cabinet ministers as Liberals pledge election review
item.description: As SA Liberal leader Ashton Hurn pledged to review the weekend's catastrophic defeat for her party, the Labor caucus met for the first time since its resounding win, with the premier hitting back at remarks made by One Nation's Pauline Hanson.

item.title: Trial begins for Townsville mother charged with deaths of sons
item.description: Leeanne Eatts is alleged to have omitted her duty to supervise the children who drowned in a river.

item.title: Former mayor of Lismore Jenny Dowell dies aged 75
item.description: The prime minister has paid tribute to the former Labor member and regional mayor, hailing her as a trailblazer.

item.title: Tourists cancelling bookings, freight costs increasing, some service stations running out of fuel
item.description: Rising fuel costs and concerns about supply affect the wider Tasmanian economy, and some service stations run out of unleaded petrol.

item.title: Alleged petrol station thief Glenn McCagh in court after servo worker seriously injured in hit-and-run
item.description: An alleged fuel thief accused of seriously injuring a Perth petrol station worker in a hit-and-run is told he will likely go to prison if convicted of the "extremely serious" charges.

item.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle set to impact Perth over weekend, with heavy rainfall expected
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Narelle is set to impact Perth and towns north of the city over the weekend with heavy rainfall expected.

item.title: Brisbane Broncos 'surprised' as Ben Te'o resigns after dispute with coach Michael Maguire
item.description: Brisbane defensive coach Ben Te'o, the mastermind behind the scrambling defence in the 2025 grand final win, hands in his resignation.

item.title: Accused Easey Street killer Perry Kouroumblis to face rape charge
item.description: Prosecutors have reinstated a rape charge against accused Easey Street killer Perry Kouroumblis after a magistrate had earlier struck out the offence.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Gold Coast Suns's Bailey Humphrey fails at AFL tribunal to get dangerous tackle ban overturned
item.description: Young gun Bailey Humphrey will miss Gold Coast's next two matches through suspension, after the AFL tribunal rejects his arguments against a rough conduct ban.

item.title: Former defence leaders outline already-present fossil fuel dependence, climate disinformation threats
item.description: Australians are already deeply emersed in a global disinformation war about fossil fuels and climate change, former defence leaders say in an extensive new review.

item.title: Donald Trump backs down on threats to Iran
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Trump Organization paid for Gold Coast Mayor Tom Tate's meals and more during Mar-a-lago stay
item.description: The Gold Coast mayor visited the luxurious estate days before an Australian developer signed a deal to build a Trump-branded tower at Surfers Paradise.

item.title: Married at First Sight expert Mel Schilling dies, aged 54
item.description: Schilling's husband, Gareth Brisbane, took to social media on Tuesday night to reveal the news.

item.title: Israel plans to occupy southern Lebanon from the Blue Line to the Litani River
item.description: Israel's defence minister has announced plans for his country's military to take control of southern Lebanon, from the Israeli border — known as the Blue Line — to the Litani River.

item.title: Strikes to water and energy infrastructure in Middle East a 'war crime'
item.description: A former Iranian environment boss says strikes, and threatened strikes, on energy and water facilities across the Middle East are on a scale never seen before.

item.title: The Kates, Kate McLennan and Kate McCartney, take crime comedy Deadloch to Darwin
item.description: Kate McLennan and Kate McCartney (Get Krack!n) set out to make a "funny Broadchurch" with crime comedy Deadloch. Now, with fans around the world, it's back for season two.

item.title: Travel quiz, March 25: Do you have the stamina to complete this trip?
item.description: Which country has the dong as its currency? And where would you dine on tteokbokki?

item.title: Did AI really take their jobs?
item.description: As big tech companies sack staff and say AI will pick up the slack, workers are left to grapple with what's really going on.

item.title: Young, unhoused — but hopeful: Ambitions for life beyond homelessness
item.description: Spare bedrooms, couches, and cars hide the stories of Australia's youth homeless crisis. But they are stories that also reveal a grit and ambition to defy the statistics.

item.title: NDIS participants work more, feel better after accessing scheme, report finds
item.description: New analysis by the e61 Institute, which tracks NDIS participants over more than a decade, finds substantial improvements to their workforce participation and self-reported energy levels within four years of accessing the taxpayer-funded scheme.

item.title: Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke admits luck — not a plan — saved lives in Perth Australia Day failed bomb attack
item.description: The home affairs minister says Australia was "so lucky" to avoid mass casualties during the attempted Australia Day bomb attack in Perth and admits the nation was slow to recognise the gravity of the alleged terrorist act.

item.title: Waste collectors warn bin services may stop if diesel not found urgently
item.description: Waste collectors say they are struggling to acquire diesel and many are operating at "unsustainable" losses, putting the collection of household bins and commercial waste under threat.

item.title: Donald Trump says Iran sent US 'big present' as Pentagon readies thousands more troops for Middle East
item.description: The US president suggests a mystery gift "worth a tremendous amount" is aiding diplomatic efforts to end the war, and comes as he reportedly prepares to send a major airborne force to the region.

item.title: The Malinauskas government's second term could be harder than its first
item.description: Despite holding an overwhelming majority in parliament, the Malinauskas government faces no shortage of policy and political challenges to navigate in its second term.

item.title: First Western Sydney Airport international flight tickets go on sale
item.description: Singapore Airlines put their first international flights from WSI on sale this morning, confirming the airline will be among the first to run from Sydney's new airport.

item.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Matthew Doran
item.description: The focus of the Middle East war shifts to Lebanon as Israel plans to occupy southern parts of the country.

item.title: Conor Leahy claims individual pursuit gold at Australian track cycling nationals
item.description: Fresh from helping Oliver Bleddyn to overall victory in the ProVelo Super League on the road, Olympic champion Conor Leahy turned from domestique to dominant victor as he claimed a sixth Australian individual pursuit title at the track cycling national championships.

item.title: E-bike changes coming to NSW include reduced power and proposed age limit
item.description: The NSW government has recently announced staged reforms to improve safety for e-bike riders and pedestrians. This is what it'll mean for the community.

item.title: Wine growers fear smoke taint will spoil Victorian harvest as planned burns begin
item.description: Just months after devastating bushfires, wine growers are concerned planned burns in regional Victoria pose a new risk to their grapes.

item.title: Former nurse Sarah Mullally tasked with healing a wounded Anglican Church
item.description: When Sarah Mullally is installed as the leader of the Church of England this week, many senior clerics won't be in attendance. For some, it's because she's a woman.

item.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle re-forms off WA, expected to reach category four
item.description: Having cut a swathe across northern Australia over the past week, Tropical Cyclone Narelle is now threatening coastal communities in Western Australia.

item.title: Live: Iran fires missiles across Middle East after Strait of Hormuz co-ordination offer
item.description: Several countries across the Middle East confirm Iran has launched drone and missile strikes across the region. It comes after Iran told the UN that "non-hostile vessels" may pass through the Strait of Hormuz if they coordinate with Iranian authorities. Follow live.

item.title: Russia fires nearly 400 drones in deadly attack on Ukraine, with UNESCO site targeted
item.description: Three were killed and 30 wounded in a rare daytime attack, including on the city of Lviv, while four have been killed in an overnight bombardment.

item.title: 'She Matters' mural honouring victims of gender violence defaced in Orange for third time
item.description: A group of "very young kids" is suspected of being behind the defacement of a mural in the NSW Central West that honours 166 victims of gender violence.

item.title: Location of Brisbane's Victoria Park stadium for 2032 Olympic and Paralympic Games unveiled
item.description: New artist impressions showing the interior of the 63,000-seat arena have also been released.

item.title: Alleged northern NSW bank robber arrested after 30 years on the run
item.description: A man wanted by police over two bank robberies in NSW almost 30 years ago has been arrested and charged.

item.title: Three people killed, two injured in crash involving car and truck near Birdwood
item.description: The sedan driver had life-threatening injuries and a fourth passenger was seriously injured.

item.title: Football Australia vows high-quality Matildas opponents in lead-up to 2027 Women's World Cup
item.description: After the highs of their run to the Asian Cup final, in just over two weeks the Matildas travel to Kenya to face teams ranked outside the top 100.

item.title: Third man charged over the alleged abduction and murder of Chris Baghsarian
item.description: Police allege the 23-year-old man was a "major player" in organising the abduction plot.

item.title: Live: Australian share market rises ahead of CPI data release
item.description: Australian shares have opened higher, after a volatile session on Wall Street where investors swayed between fears of rising oil prices and hopes for a resolution to the US-Israeli war on Iran. Follow the latest updates in our live blog.

item.title: Mo Salah announces he will leave Liverpool at end of EPL season
item.description: Mo Salah announces he is leaving Liverpool after almost a decade of starring for the EPL giants.

item.title: Woman dies after being struck by truck in North West Queensland
item.description: A woman dies after being hit by a truck on the Barkly Highway in North West Queensland.

item.title: Cacti confiscated from home garden over fears of another 'prickly pear' disaster
item.description: An opal miner's garden plants have been destroyed after it was identified as a prohibited plant, potentially as invasive as one of Australia's most notorious plants.

item.title: Federal and state governments announce $2b bailout for Rio Tinto's Boyne aluminium smelter
item.description: Rio Tinto, the federal and state governments have struck a landmark partnership to secure the long-term future of the Boyne aluminium smelter.

item.title: Michael Atherton, Michael Vaughan and Jonny Bairstow question England's Ashes review
item.description: Former England captains and current player Jonny Bairstow question the findings of an internal review into a disastrous Ashes campaign, which saw the team's leaders maintain their positions.

item.title: Cyclone Narelle's trajectory puts Western Australia on hit list
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Narelle is now barrelling towards Western Australia. Could this unusual cyclone make it all the way to Perth, and how does climate change affect it?

item.title: Basil Zempilas says he is 'not fearful' of One Nation's rise ahead of the 2029 WA election
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: WA Opposition Leader Basil Zempilas 'not fearful' of One Nation's rise ahead of 2029 WA election
item.description: Basil Zempilas is "not fearful" of the soaring rise of Pauline Hanson's One Nation in the west, but acknowledges his WA Liberals will need to decide whether to direct preference votes to the controversial party or distance themselves.

item.title: Cyclone Narelle tracker map: Where is it forecast to cross WA coast?
item.description: Track the path of Tropical Cyclone Narelle as it heads towards the WA coast.

item.title: Major facelift proposed for Mount Gambier's sunken Umpherston Sinkhole garden
item.description: The Mount Gambier council is asking for feedback on a significant change to the regional city's beloved sunken garden.

item.title: Katherine River could hit major flood level again as BOM warns of lingering ex-TC Narelle impacts
item.description: Residents in Katherine are bracing for the possibility of major riverine flooding today, which would mark the second significant weather event to rock the town in a month.

item.title: Police investigate suspicious fire at heritage-listed Kilmore church being converted into a mosque
item.description: Police are investigating a fire at a 160-year-old heritage-listed church north of Melbourne that was being converted into a mosque by the local Muslim community.

item.title: Tom Gleeson, Aaron Chen, Paul McDermott and more on 40 years of the Melbourne Comedy Festival
item.description: The very first Melbourne International Comedy Festival launched 40 years ago, and while it's kept its scrappy charm, things look pretty different today.

item.title: Couples on sleeping separately and if it's helped — or hindered — their relationships
item.description: Snoring, shift work, parenting and health — you shared the many reasons why you no longer sleep in the same bed as your partner, and whether it's been a positive move for sleep and the relationship.

item.title: Bulk carrier that escaped Iranian blockade of Strait of Hormuz due to arrive in Australia
item.description: The Japanese-owned Iron Maiden will be the first known ship to dock in Australia after it was stranded in the Persian Gulf when the US and Israel began bombing Iran on February 28.

item.title: Man accused of raping woman in her apartment testifies 'she wasn't that drunk'
item.description: Panormitis Charalampis, co-accused of picking up an intoxicated young woman from outside a nightclub while she was waiting for an uber and sexually assaulting her at her apartment, took the stand, undergoing hours of intense cross-examination.

item.title: Jetstar reduces flights between New Zealand and Australia due to rising jet fuel prices
item.description: Jetstar announces it is reducing flights between Australia and New Zealand due to the rising cost of jet fuel as a result of the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Browse Island mouse baiting.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Drone baiting aims to eradicate infestation of house mice from remote WA island
item.description: Scientists are using "world-first" drone-baiting technology to target thousands of house mice in an effort to bring migratory seabirds back from the brink on Browse Island off Western Australia's north.

item.title: Married at First Sight expert Mel Schilling remembered as 'amazing' and 'radiant' after bowel cancer death
item.description: Mel Schilling became one of Australian TV's most recognised relationship experts, but who was she, and what led to her death?

item.title: ABC journalists and staff begin 24-hour strike action after rejecting enterprise agreement offer
item.description: A majority of staff who voted rejected the broadcaster's latest pay offer on the three-year enterprise bargaining agreement, which paved the way for the strike action.

item.title: Government talks down fuel rationing, as Chalmers concedes 5 per cent inflation scenario 'conservative'
item.description: The government has requested new Treasury modelling for "more challenging circumstances" stemming from the Middle East war, admitting that the economic scenarios released last week "look pretty conservative now".

item.title: Man dies in NT police custody after arrest and detainment in police car cage in Darwin
item.description: A man has died in police custody in Darwin's northern suburbs overnight, with police saying he was "arrested without incident".

item.title: Inflation cooled slightly in February before energy price rise caused by Iran war
item.description: The latest monthly data does not capture the recent rise in energy prices caused by the war in the Middle East, which began on February 28.

item.title: WA public servant allegedly helped intimate partner skip social housing waitlist
item.description: A 55-year-old public servant is accused of illegally offering a social housing tenancy to her intimate partner, allowing them to skip a large number of applicants who were in the queue for a home.

item.title: Regional WA shire agrees to sell $22m workers' camp to Westgold Resources
item.description: It was supposed to be a cash cow amid yet another mining boom, but the Shire of Coolgardie is selling its controversial Bluebush Village for $22.05 million, as part of plans to clear massive debts.

item.title: Danish election bruises Mette Frederiksen's Social Democrats amid fights over immigration and Greenland
item.description: Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen's Social Democrats look set to suffer the party's worst election outcome in more than a century.

item.title: Waqas Haider charged with murder over Monaro Highway crash that killed Bega valley woman
item.description: A 40-year-old Canberra man has been charged with murder after a crash that killed a Bega Valley woman on the Monaro Highway in early March.

item.title: OpenAI pulls plug on AI video app Sora that sparked deepfake concerns
item.description: Sora was released in September by the company behind ChatGPT and soon went viral as a place to share short-form videos generated by artificial intelligence, but it also raised alarms.

item.title: Hong Kong police arrest bookstore owner and staff for allegedly selling Jimmy Lai biography, broadcaster reports
item.description: Hong Kong police have arrested a bookstore owner and three shopkeepers for allegedly selling "seditious" publications.

item.title: $50 million to upgrade detour roads during Great Western Highway's closure
item.description: Testing confirms a Great Western Highway bridge remains 'unstable' as $50 million is announced to upgrade three key detours through the Blue Mountains.

item.title: Wallabies flyhalf Tom Lynagh returns for Reds, Zac Lomax joins Western Force teammates
item.description: The Queensland Reds are attempting to achieve their first Super Rugby win in Wellington since 1998, and their maiden win at a ground known as the Cake Tin.

item.title: Trump administration offers 15-point ceasefire plan to Iran
item.description: The ceasefire plan comes as the US military prepares to call up at least 1,000 more troops to supplement those already in the Middle East.

item.title: Democrats flip seat in Florida district home to Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort
item.description: Democrats celebrate the victory as the latest sign voters are turning against Donald Trump and Republicans ahead of November's midterm elections.

item.title: Shorter showers, reduced flights and no street lights among measures to address Asia's energy crisis
item.description: Countries across Asia are moving to protect their energy supplies after Iran effectively blockaded the crucial Strait of Hormuz passage in response to the United States and Israel's attacks.

item.title: International bodies back Rockhampton for 2032 Olympic Games rowing and canoeing
item.description: It comes as the Queensland government reveals the preferred course options for a controversial central Queensland river chosen as a venue for the 2032 Olympic Games.

item.title: Meta ordered to pay $375m in US trial over child exploitation, user safety claims
item.description: The verdict marks the first time a jury has ruled on such claims against Meta, as the company faces a wave of lawsuits over how its platforms affect young people's mental health.

item.title: McEvoy decries 'ludicrous' situation where Enhanced Games doping pays better than legitimate swimming records
item.description: Australian swimmer and 50m freestyle world record holder Cam McEvoy says it is "ludicrous" that doped-up swimmers are being offered as much as $2 million to break a world record, while his legitimate mark earned him nothing.
